title: Startups Brag They Spend More Money on AI Than Human Employees
summary: A new class of AI startups say they are taking money that would normally be used to hire people and are spending it on AI compute instead.
reason: This is a concrete, visible moment in the capture sequence where the alibi of AI-as-tool collapses into AI-as-replacement, exposing the routing architecture that redirects value flows away from labor and toward compute infrastructure.
angle: Use routing architecture analysis: these startups aren't just automating tasks, they're re-routing the wage relation itself — value that would flow through workers now flows through compute providers, restructuring who can participate in production and on what terms.

title: After Three-Week Strike, JBS Concedes to Meatpacking Workers
summary: Last month, 3,800 meatpacking workers in UFCW Local 7 in Greeley, Colorado launched the industry’s first major strike in 40 years. The three-week unfair labor practice strike was the first time workers had ever struck the JBS Greeley beef packing plant, one of the company’s largest. ULP charges against JBS included the illegal termination of a member of the bargaining committee and surveillance and intimidation of workers for participation in union activity.
reason: A successful strike against one of the world's largest meat processors—won on ULP charges around surveillance and illegal termination—shows the commune layer (collective labor action) generating leverage that market and legal mechanisms alone couldn't produce.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: the strike rerouted productive power away from JBS's capture circuit and back through the workers' collective organization, making visible how the plant's profitability depends on a social substrate—solidarity, shared knowledge of rights, collective will—that JBS cannot manufacture or replace.

title: How Rhode Island’s Labor Movement Made Its Weight Felt in Politics
summary: Over the last decade Rhode Island has been a hotbed of progressive, pro-worker legislation. But it wasn’t always this way. It took years of proactive organizing by the labor movement on legislative and electoral campaigns. This “blue” New England state was led by Republican Governor Donald Carcieri from 2003 to 2011. During his term he cut 1,000 public sector jobs, passed a regressive property tax law, and attacked pensions for teachers and other public workers—actions that were enabled by centrist Democrats in the state legislature who were lukewarm towards labor.

title: Camping Out a Chemical Giant: Diane Wilson Persists in Hunger Strike for Clean Waters
summary: The fourth-generation fisherwoman from Texas’ Gulf Coast said she won’t let Dow continue to pollute the community’s bays and waterways.
reason: A fisherwoman defending a commons (shared waterways) against industrial enclosure by a chemical giant is a direct example of the commune layer under attack and prefigurative resistance to extraction.
angle: Use the 'commune layer' and 'enclosure' tools: the bays and waterways are a productive commons that Dow extracts from while externalizing costs onto the community that depends on them, and Wilson's body-as-blockade is a routing-level intervention, not merely symbolic.

title: What Organizers Today Can Learn From the Socialist Jewish Labor Bund Movement
summary: Artist and journalist Molly Crabapple discusses the lessons she learned while writing her new book “Here Where We Live Is Our Country: The Story of the Jewish Bund.”
reason: The Bund represents a historical case of prefigurative institution-building — building parallel infrastructures of care, education, and mutual aid that routed flows outside both state and capital — directly illuminating the commune layer and routing architecture concepts.
angle: Use the Bund as a historical example of flow-register organizing: not just political messaging but the construction of alternative productive circuits (schools, unions, cultural institutions) that routed solidarity through the community rather than through market or state intermediaries.

angle: Use the commune layer tool to show how letter carriers are already running a distributed care and knowledge network through neighborhoods, and the routing architecture question of what gets destroyed if that network is captured or dismantled.

title: The week that Meta employees became training data
summary: Invasive monitoring and a fresh round of layoffs have workers I spoke to on edge. Is this the future of knowledge work?
reason: Meta's surveillance and layoff regime illustrates the capture sequence operating on its own workforce — workers who built the platform's common substrate of social knowledge are now being extracted from and replaced by the outputs of that extraction.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to show how Meta is rerouting the productive circuit — previously: worker knowledge → platform value; now: worker knowledge → training data → model → worker elimination — making the epistemic commons built by labor into the instrument of its own enclosure.
